verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the beta subunit of ATP synthase, a core component of the inner mitochondrial membrane (Complex V). It is synthesized in the cytosol without a signal peptide, imported into mitochondria via TOM/TIM machinery, and remains embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane facing the matrix. No transmembrane domain spans the plasma membrane; no documented secretion, shedding, or surface-targeting isoforms; no PM cycling or dual localization; no documented cancer / cell-death / activation / germline ecto-pool; not a clinical surface-directed target. Unambiguously mitochondrial-internal.
